What should they do now? Should they seek aid, prove worthy before that regard, or listen for the wisdom hidden beneath the calm?

encounterOptions.label: Ask The Shepherd Of Trees
encounterOptions.description: Appeal to Treebeard for the rooted strength, stubborn endurance, and living force of Fangorn.

outcomes.resultText: Treebeard answers in a voice deep as old bark, and when he turns his strength toward you it feels as though the forest itself has decided not to let you fail.
He gives only a slow, uncertain courtesy, as if your need were too hasty and too small to move an old heart burdened by longer griefs.

encounterOptions.label: Stand Before The Old Ent
encounterOptions.description: Hold your ground beneath the weight of his age and prove you are not one more passing fool with a sharp tool and a short memory.

outcomes.resultText: You endure the long scrutiny of Treebeard without shrinking. At last the old Ent acknowledges something sound in you, and the honor of that regard strengthens your heart.
The measure of his age and sorrow proves too much for you. Before Treebeard's deep judgment, your strength buckles and leaves you diminished.

encounterOptions.label: Listen To The Long Memory
encounterOptions.description: Attend to his pauses, his anger, and the creak of the world in him until some older wisdom finally comes clear.

outcomes.resultText: At last you hear the old understanding beneath his slowness: grief stored in wood-rings, wrath held in root, and wisdom grown patient through centuries. The lesson settles in you like deep earth.
His thought runs too deep and too long for you to follow. You leave with more weight in your mind than wisdom in your hand.

fleeOption.label: Leave The Ent Moot Unjoined
fleeOption.description: Withdraw before Treebeard's long judgment roots you in place.

outcomes.resultText: You leave Treebeard behind, but the slow gravity of his presence clings to you and turns your steps heavy.

cards.name: Vampire
cards.description: An encounter with a creature of the night can be investigated.
cards.historyText:
The cold in that place did not belong to weather, but to old earth, shut stones, and names that had not rested properly.

When the thing before him stirred, the dread came with it, and suddenly the grave no longer felt far below but terribly near.

What should they do now? Should they strike first, slip from the dead things reach, or risk words with what should have remained silent?

encounterOptions.label: Defy The Grave-Hunger
encounterOptions.description: Strike before the vampire can turn darkness, fear, and unnatural hunger into one perfect kill.

outcomes.resultText: You break the vampire's predatory poise and wrench grim reward from an encounter steeped in death and hunger.
Though you survive, the vampire leaves wounds of fear and flesh that feel older than the night itself.

encounterOptions.label: Slip The Night-Wing Hunt
encounterOptions.description: Move before the creature's nocturnal patience can tighten around your breath, blood, and path.

outcomes.resultText: You pass beyond the vampire's hungriest reach before its night-born pursuit can wholly claim you.
You get away, but the chill of the hunt leaves your retreat slow and deeply shaken.

encounterOptions.label: Speak To The Undead Appetite
encounterOptions.description: Try dark courtesy, tribute, or kinship of evil and see whether the creature's hunger can be diverted for a moment.

outcomes.resultText: Something in your darkness or cunning stays the vampire's hunger long enough for you to pass outside its immediate claim.
The vampire hears no language dearer than fear, and that is all it truly draws from you.

fleeOption.label: Flee
fleeOption.description: Run before the night's hunger fixes fully on you and strips even retreat of hope.

outcomes.resultText: You escape the Vampire, but only after its grave-cold pursuit has robbed your retreat of steadiness.

cards.name: Vision Of Luthien
cards.description: An encounter with a radiant vision can be investigated.
cards.historyText:
The place and the meeting alike carried the feeling that ordinary rules had already thinned or slipped aside.

What stood before him was not easily measured, and the mind kept reaching for familiar explanations only to find that none of them quite held.

What should they do now? Should they press forward into the wonder, move carefully around the peril, or search for the meaning hidden inside the strangeness?

encounterOptions.label: Ask Grace Of The Moonlit Song
encounterOptions.description: Open yourself to the vision and beg some fragment of its mercy, beauty, or strength for the road ahead.

outcomes.resultText: The vision's song passes through you like silver fire, leaving hope renewed and your path strangely light beneath your feet.
The beauty of the vision touches you only lightly before it passes, leaving more longing than comfort in its wake.

encounterOptions.label: Stand Before The Deathless Memory
encounterOptions.description: Let the vision measure your heart and see whether courage alone is enough to endure such radiant sorrow.

outcomes.resultText: You do not falter before the majesty of the vision, and the strength it awakens in you is noble, fierce, and clean.
Before such beauty and loss, your spirit fails to hold its full ground, and the ache of that failing cuts deep.

encounterOptions.label: Read The Spell Beneath The Song
encounterOptions.description: Listen past wonder into the old power, grief, and defiance hidden in the vision's radiance.

outcomes.resultText: You glimpse in the vision not only beauty, but the ancient force by which darkness itself was once brought low. The insight burns bright and clear.
The vision is too fair and too deep, and you come away only dazed by beauty you could not truly understand.

fleeOption.label: Flee
fleeOption.description: Turn aside before the vision can draw your heart further into longing and remembrance.

outcomes.resultText: You leave the vision behind, yet its song lingers painfully sweet in memory long after the road has darkened again.

cards.name: War Chief From Rhun
cards.description: An encounter with an eastern war-chief can be investigated.
cards.historyText:
A chill of command walked before the figure, and by the time they stood face to face with it the world already felt narrower, harsher, and less forgiving.

Here was power shaped by fear, discipline, and long intention, the sort that rarely offers anything without claiming something in return.

What should they do now? Should they confront the menace outright, evade the will pressing upon him, or answer in the language of rank, threat, or shadow?

encounterOptions.label: Break The Eastern Warlord
encounterOptions.description: Attack before the war chief's discipline and violence can settle the whole encounter into his kind of battle.

outcomes.resultText: You shatter the Rhunic chief's battle-poise and take hard reward from a victory won against iron discipline.
Though you survive, the war chief's steel and command leave you scarred with fear and hurt alike.

encounterOptions.label: Slip The Rhunic Battle-Sense
encounterOptions.description: Move before the war chief can read the field fully and turn your every path into one more trap of war.

outcomes.resultText: You evade the war chief's strongest reading of the field and pass out of his best killing line just in time.
You escape the war chief's full hold, but the strain of the encounter breaks your retreat into a slower, rougher thing.

encounterOptions.label: Answer The Iron East
encounterOptions.description: Use rank, threat, or darker allegiance and see whether the Rhunic chief judges you worth sparing.

outcomes.resultText: The war chief hears enough purpose or allegiance in your answer to let the meeting pass without immediate bloodshed.
Your words win nothing from the Rhunic chief, and fear takes hold as surely as if a spear-point had found your throat.

fleeOption.label: Flee
fleeOption.description: Break away before eastern discipline and violence turn your retreat into one more defeated campaign.

outcomes.resultText: You escape the War Chief From Rhun, but only after the pressure of that hard command has left you slowed and unsettled.

cards.name: Warden Of Lothlorien
cards.description: An encounter with an elven warden can be investigated.
cards.historyText:
The meeting came in a place where light, leaf, and stillness seemed to have agreed on some older order long before he arrived.

Whoever stood before him belonged to that order with effortless grace, and the feeling of being weighed, tested, or quietly measured was impossible to ignore.

What should they do now? Should they seek aid, prove worthy before that regard, or listen for the wisdom hidden beneath the calm?

encounterOptions.label: Ask Leave Of The Golden Wood
encounterOptions.description: Declare your purpose plainly and ask the warden for shelter, guidance, or passage beneath guarded boughs.

outcomes.resultText: The warden accepts your purpose and grants what aid he may beneath the eaves of Lothlorien. For a little while, the Golden Wood itself seems to speed and hearten you.
His gaze remains cool and unreadable, and no welcome truly opens before you. You go on beneath the uneasy feeling of having been suffered, not trusted.

encounterOptions.label: Stand The Warden's Test
encounterOptions.description: Let him measure your bearing as one who may deserve to walk unharmed through a realm of hidden sentinels.

outcomes.resultText: You do not shrink beneath the warden's searching eyes, and at last he grants the grave respect reserved for those who do not profane the Golden Wood.
He sees at once what in you does not belong among these trees, and the sting of that judgment cuts deeper than a drawn arrow.

encounterOptions.label: Read The Silent Bow-Paths
encounterOptions.description: Study his watchfulness, the forest around him, and the concealed order by which Lothlorien guards its own.

outcomes.resultText: You begin to understand how the wardens hear danger in silence and read trespass in the trembling of leaves. The Golden Wood yields a sliver of its guarded wisdom.
The paths of the wardens remain hidden from you, and the wood seems only more secretive for having tried to read it.

fleeOption.label: Flee
fleeOption.description: Withdraw before the golden silence closes more tightly around you.

outcomes.resultText: You leave the warden and the Golden Wood behind, though its watchful stillness follows you for some distance like an unseen escort.

cards.name: Warg King
cards.description: An encounter with a beast-lord can be investigated.
cards.historyText:
Before they fully saw it, they felt the living danger of it: hunger, motion, and the sense that the ground itself had tilted toward tooth, sting, fang, or coil.

Whatever stood before him belonged more to instinct than mercy, and yet it carried its own dreadful kind of purpose.

What should they do now? Should they meet the creature by force, evade its reach, or try some desperate answer before it closes in?

encounterOptions.label: Break The Pack-Lord
encounterOptions.description: Throw your strength at the Warg King before his pack can fully close and pull you down together.

outcomes.resultText: You shatter the charge of the pack-lord and take hard profit from a victory won beneath snapping death.
Though you escape the kill, the Warg King's bite and terror mark you before he yields the field.

encounterOptions.label: Slip The Hunting Circle
encounterOptions.description: Use speed, scent-breaking cunning, and desperate timing before the pack closes its full ring around you.

outcomes.resultText: You evade the Warg King's tightening hunt and vanish before his pack can drag you into its ring.
Though you escape the pack-lord's jaws, the chase breaks your rhythm and leaves you lagging.

encounterOptions.label: Snarl The Dark Command
encounterOptions.description: Try command, threat, or the authority of darker service and see whether the beast-lord yields an opening.

outcomes.resultText: The Warg King senses enough darkness or dominance in you to rein in the kill for a moment and let you pass.
No word can outweigh the blood-hunger in the pack-lord's eyes. Fear rushes in before the first leap.

fleeOption.label: Flee
fleeOption.description: Run before the Warg King can bring the whole hunt down on your trail.

outcomes.resultText: You escape the Warg King, but only after the terror of the hunt leaves you slowed and ragged.

cards.name: Wight
cards.description: An encounter with an unquiet dead can be investigated.
cards.historyText:
The cold in that place did not belong to weather, but to old earth, shut stones, and names that had not rested properly.

When the thing before him stirred, the dread came with it, and suddenly the grave no longer felt far below but terribly near.

What should they do now? Should they strike first, slip from the dead things reach, or risk words with what should have remained silent?

encounterOptions.label: Break The Grave-Chill
encounterOptions.description: Strike before the dead thing's cold presence can sap your strength and root your feet to the darkened ground.

outcomes.resultText: You force your way through the grave-chill and wrench a hard-won gain from a thing that should have yielded nothing but death.
Though you survive, the touch of the wight leaves a cold dread in body and mind alike.

encounterOptions.label: Slip The Dead Hand
encounterOptions.description: Move before the unquiet dead can fully fasten its will and hunger upon you.

outcomes.resultText: You evade the wight's full grasp and pass from its reach before the chill can become a binding.
The wight's lingering cold drags at your limbs even after you have escaped its immediate reach.

encounterOptions.label: Whisper To The Unquiet Dead
encounterOptions.description: Try dark speech, deceit, or a show of kinship with the grave and see whether it buys a moment's release.

outcomes.resultText: Something in your manner stills the wight's hunger just long enough for you to pass outside its claim.
Your words vanish into the grave-cold, and all that answers is fear sharpened by death.

fleeOption.label: Flee
fleeOption.description: Turn and run before the wight's chill settles too deep to escape at all.

outcomes.resultText: You flee the wight's presence, but the dead cold of it follows long enough to ruin your pace and nerve.

cards.name: Wizard From The East
cards.description: An encounter with a wandering wizard can be investigated.
cards.historyText:
At the edge of twilight they found a figure who seemed less like a traveler than a keeper of meanings half-hidden from ordinary folk.

The air around the meeting felt full of counsel, judgment, and the sense that what was spoken here might matter long afterward.

What should they do now? Should they ask for aid, prove worthy of deeper counsel, or watch for the lesson behind the silence?

encounterOptions.label: Ask The Eastern Counsel
encounterOptions.description: Place your need before the wandering wizard and ask for some hard-won guidance from the far countries he has walked.

outcomes.resultText: Wizard From The East speaks few words, but each falls with the weight of a patient strategy long in the making. His counsel steadies you and quickens your next steps.
He gives you only a measured warning, as if you have not yet earned the fuller map of dangers he carries. You leave uneasy, knowing he withheld much.

encounterOptions.label: Stand Before The Blue Wanderer
encounterOptions.description: Show that your will can bear the quiet scrutiny of one who has spent ages opposing darkness beyond all witness.

outcomes.resultText: Wizard From The East finds no weakness in your bearing. In his calm acknowledgment there is a stern encouragement, like being admitted to a hidden labor of great consequence.
His silence weighs heavier than rebuke. Beneath that patient eastern wisdom your certainty unravels, and the encounter leaves you wounded in spirit and flesh.

encounterOptions.label: Read The Long Design
encounterOptions.description: Watch the wizard, his tokens, and his withheld words until the shape of his deeper purpose begins to emerge.

outcomes.resultText: At last you glimpse the hidden architecture of his labor: resistance woven through patience, secrecy, and distant hope. The understanding makes you subtler and harder to deceive.
His silences lead you down too many possible meanings. When you part from him, your thoughts are heavier than when you arrived.

fleeOption.label: Pass Him By
fleeOption.description: Leave before his calm scrutiny and hidden counsels can draw you into deeper matters.

outcomes.resultText: You move on from Wizard From The East, but the feeling remains that you have walked away from a warning meant especially for you.

cards.name: Wizard Of Many Colours
cards.description: An encounter with a fallen wizard can be investigated.
cards.historyText:
At the edge of twilight they found a figure who seemed less like a traveler than a keeper of meanings half-hidden from ordinary folk.

The air around the meeting felt full of counsel, judgment, and the sense that what was spoken here might matter long afterward.

What should they do now? Should they ask for aid, prove worthy of deeper counsel, or watch for the lesson behind the silence?

encounterOptions.label: Ask The Many-Coloured Counsel
encounterOptions.description: Invite the transformed wizard to lay some portion of his immense craft and influence behind your purpose.

outcomes.resultText: Wizard Of Many Colours answers with dazzling certainty, and for a moment his many-layered genius seems to bend the world itself into readiness for your cause.
He gives you words bright as polished gems, but each one leaves a hidden barb behind. When you depart, unease has taken the place of comfort.

encounterOptions.label: Stand Before The Shattered White
encounterOptions.description: Hold yourself steady before his transformed majesty and prove you can endure the force of his altered will.

outcomes.resultText: Wizard Of Many Colours studies you through all his splendid masks and finds you equal to the moment. His approval is dangerous, but it strikes like a draught of living fire.
His many-hued will crashes over you like a storm of voices and colors. When it passes, you are left diminished and shaken.

encounterOptions.label: Read The Broken Brilliance
encounterOptions.description: Study the colors, the voice, and the layered pride in him until the deeper danger behind the beauty becomes plain.

outcomes.resultText: At last you see how wisdom, pride, and hunger have been braided together inside him. The sight is perilous, but it teaches sharply.
You look too long into his glittering depths and come away carrying only disquiet, as though his brilliance had stained your thoughts.

fleeOption.label: Turn From The Many Hues
fleeOption.description: Break away before his layered splendor can draw you deeper into its danger.

outcomes.resultText: You leave Wizard Of Many Colours behind, but the memory of his glittering power clings like a temptation not fully refused.
